geosR 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • geosR Tutorial: Resource Estimation

    Resource Calculation & Evaluation

    calculators.Rd

    Resource Calculation & Evaluation

    Calculates the volume, grade, and metal content (tonnage) for a resource block. This calculation is generalized for any commodity, utilizing geometric volume and grade block models.

    Compares the calculated resource model against actual production (realization) values to generate a recovery factor or "Koefisien Hasil".

    Uses tmap to generate standardized spatial mapping plots, such as tonnage distribution over a block.

    calc_res(raster_grade, raster_thickness, area, density = 1)

ev_rest(res_table, actual_production)

plot_res(
  tonnage_raster,
  area,
  title = "Resource Estimation",
  subtitle = "",
  col_palette = "Spectral"
)

    Arguments

    raster_grade

    A SpatRaster containing the estimated grade values.

    raster_thickness

    A SpatRaster containing the estimated thickness values.

    area

    An sf polygon outlining the resource boundary.

    density

    Numeric, the bulk density or specific gravity of the material (default = 1.0).

    res_table

    A data.frame or tibble, typically the table output from calc_res().

    actual_production

    Numeric, the actual observed production values.

    tonnage_raster

    A SpatRaster representing the calculated metal content or resources per grid cell.

    title

    Character, main title for the plot.

    subtitle

    Character, subtitle or panel label content (e.g., total calculated resources).

    col_palette

    Character, color palette for the raster mapping. Default is "Spectral".

    Value

    A list containing the raster of calculated metal content (tonnage) and a table summarizing the area, expected volume, average grade, and total metal content per polygon.

    The original resource table with appended columns for actual_production and recovery_factor.

    A tmap plot object suitable for viewing or export.

    Details

    These functions provide the core tools for computing block volumes, applying density parameters for tonnages, evaluating recovery factors against realization, and plotting the resulting resource maps.

    Contents

    Developed by Ghozian Karami.
